title: FOIA US Navy record  5/18 Vietnam War

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Walter Gouveia
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: Sept 20, 1936
Dates of Service: Approx 1955 to 1980
passed away 2015

Request following documents;
1) copy of Bronze Star Medal award citations
2) copy of History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) copy of Navy commendation medal award citation
4)  training history NAVPERS form 601-4
5) copy of Presidential Unit Citation award
5326   
Redact per 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.
Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: National Climate Assessment (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all communications between this agency and the White House, Executive Office of the President, Office of Management and Budget, Office of Science and Technology Policy, Council on Environmental Quality, or Department of Government Efficiency concerning the National Climate Assessment, globalchange.gov, USGCRP, climate webpages, climate datasets, climate reports, or public access to climate science materials.

Please include emails, calendar invitations, attachments, memoranda, Teams/Slack messages, briefing materials, and meeting notes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: Untitled (Office of Science and Technology Policy)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records concerning legal review of the removal, disabling, discontinuation, archiving, or reduced public availability of National Climate Assessment materials, globalchange.gov, or other U.S. Global Change Research Program materials.

Please include legal memoranda, attorney-client communications where segregable factual material can be released, records discussing the Global Change Research Act of 1990, records discussing statutory obligations to produce or disseminate the National Climate Assessment, and records discussing litigation risk, records retention, the Federal Records Act, FOIA, public access, or scientific integrity obligations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: National Climate Assessment (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records showing changes to federal webpages or public-facing digital assets related to the National Climate Assessment and globalchange.gov from June 1, 2025, through July 15, 2025.

This includes content-management-system logs, GitHub or code repository commits, web publishing logs, redirect records, deletion logs, access-control changes, DNS records, web server configuration changes, help-desk tickets, Jira tickets, ServiceNow tickets, contractor work orders, and records identifying the user, office, contractor, or system account that made or approved the changes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: Removal of National Climate Assessment materials (Office of Science and Technology Policy)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all records identifying, discussing, approving, transmitting, or implementing any instruction, recommendation, order, directive, request, or guidance to remove, disable, redirect, archive, decommission, or otherwise limit public access to National Climate Assessment materials or the globalchange.gov website.

This request includes records showing the names, titles, offices, agencies, contractors, or White House components involved in making, approving, transmitting, or implementing the decision.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: Removal of National Climate Assessments (U.s. Global Change Research Program)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I would like to request records from Jan. 20, 2025, to the present concerning the removal, disabling, redirection, archiving, migration, or loss of public access to federal webpages, PDFs, portals, datasets, or other online materials related to the National Climate Assessment, including but not limited to the First, Second, Third, Fourth and Fifth National Climate Assessments and any webpages formerly available through globalchange.gov.

Please include emails, memoranda, decision documents, meeting agendas, meeting notes, talking points, directives, web tickets, content-management-system records, contracts, task orders, legal reviews, and communications with contractors.

Search terms should include: “National Climate Assessment,” “NCA,” “NCA5,” “Fifth National Climate Assessment,” “globalchange.gov,” “USGCRP,” “remove,” “removed,” “offline,” “redirect,” “archive,” “decommission,” “web content,” “website,” “public access,” “climate assessment,” and “take down.”

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lauren Pearle

title: FOIA US Army record 5/16

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA US Army record 5/16
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records concerning below:
Name: Phillip D. Clarkson
Branch: United States Army
DOB: Feb 17, 1943
Dates of Service: 1962 to 1985
passed away 2018

Please provide the following documents:

title: Communications about page removals from CFPB website

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Communications about page removals from CFPB website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

During the week of May 18, CFPB has made two significant alterations to the content of the website. First, the website removed all public speeches, press releases, transcripts and other content preceding February 2025. In addition, the website has also recently removed all non-English content. Both of these decisions impact how CFPB provides services to the public and how certain audiences (historians, economists, etc.) might have used the CFPB website to cite past evidence.

As far as I can tell, there has been no public deliberation or announcement of either of these pending changes despite their potentially wide-ranging effects. I would like to request internal communications and discussions about both of these decisions and their implementation. This will likely be during the week of May 18 or before, so a range of May 2026 might suffice. I would expect such communications to be between the CFPB director's office (also known as the Front Office) including Russ Vought or some of his deputies, the chief counsel and CFPB, representatives of public-facing functions like Consumer Education and also the technical staff responsible for the website within the Design & Development division that is part of Technology & Innovation in Operations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Harris

title: Untitled

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All body-worn camera footage from an incident occurring February 15, 2022
Location: Orlando International Airport, Orlando FL
A woman, barred from boarding her flight because she was intoxicated, cussed out a police officer and [then rode away on her motorized luggage](https://www.clickorlando.com/news/local/2022/02/15/full-video-drunken-woman-on-motorized-suitcase-leads-police-chase-through-orlando-airport-officials-say/). An officer pursued her through the airport—on a bicycle. Chelsea Alston, 32, was later accused of battering the police officer and causing more than $1,000 in damage to his patrol car, court records allege. If convicted, she faces up to five years in prison for each offense.
February 15, 2022
Location: Orlando International Airport, Orlando FL
Agency: Orlando Police Department or Aviation Authority Police

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

title: Casper PD

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wyoming Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The complete incident report, including any supplemental narratives, officer body-worn camera footage, and any associated documentation, generated in response to a call for service on May 20, 2026, at approximately 7:00 PM at or near Shepherd of the Hills Presbyterian Church, 4600 S Poplar St, Casper, WY 82601. Involved parties include ChyAnna R. Esau-Dick, DOB 4/12/1994, and her minor daughter Lennon Rae Dick, DOB 4/13/2020.
2. The audio recording of the non-emergency line call placed to the Casper Public Safety Communication Center on May 20, 2026, at approximately 7:00 PM concerning the above incident. I also request any internal radio traffic between Casper PSCC and responding officers regarding this call for service.

To the extent any portion of the requested records concerns a minor, I request that records be produced with the minor's identifying information redacted (including name, date of birth, and any other personal identifiers), rather than withheld in their entirety. The Wyoming Juvenile Justice Act, Wyo. Stat. § 14-6-201 et seq., governs records concerning juvenile offenders within the juvenile justice system and does not categorically prohibit disclosure of incident reports involving minors as incidental parties or witnesses to civil disputes. Redaction of identifying information adequately protects any minor's privacy interest in this context.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding, identify the records or portions withheld, and explain the application of the exemption, in compliance with Wyo. Stat. § 16-4-203. I reserve all rights under the Wyoming Public Records Act, including the right to seek judicial review pursuant to Wyo. Stat. § 16-4-203(g).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Vig Sinclair

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 23, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 23, 2026.

Please provide any minutes for this meeting, whether in draft or final form.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 23, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on April 6, April 27, May 4, and May 18, 2026. At the May 18, 2026 meeting, the Board approved the March 9, 2026 meeting minutes.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

communications.subject: RE: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Release of Mugshots for Damien Echols
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Pursuant to A.C.A. § 12-27-113(e)(2)(A)(i), disclosure or inspection of information contained in inmate records is prohibited unless authorized by Administrative Rule or by court order.
 Administrative Rules 804(IV) allows the following information from an inmate record to be released:
1. Offender's name, aliases, ADC number, photograph, physical description, date of birth, age, race, and gender.
2. Date of incarceration.
3. Facility(ies) of confinement, work assignments, and program participation (unless it involves drug and alcohol program participation).
4. The following information regarding current incarceration: offense (s), sentence (s), sentence date (s), county (ies) of conviction, case number (s), total time to serve, parole/transfer eligibility date, movements and behavior.
5. The following information regarding prior incarceration(s): offense(s), sentence(s), sentence date(s), county(ies) of conviction, case number(s), movements, behavior and parole/clemency history, including date(s) of release and date(s) of return to ADC.
6. Detainers.
I have attached the disclosable information responsive to your request.
Kind regards,

Miles S. Morgan


Miles Morgan
ATTORNEY III
Shared Services | Department of Corrections
e: Miles.S.Morgan@doc.arkansas.gov
t: 501-682-9540


                                CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:
This email and any attachments are the property of the State of Arkansas and may be confidential or legally privileged under applicable state and federal laws, including laws governing the disclosure of private information. They are intended solely for use by the designated recipient(s). If you are not the intended recipient, or have received this message in error, you are hereby notified that any review, use, retention, dissemination, distribution, copying, printing, or reliance on this communication is STRICTLY PROHIBITED and may be unlawful. The sender does not waive any applicable privilege by the transmission of this message. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by reply email and permanently delete all copies of this message and any attachments from your system. This email and any responses to it may be subject to disclosure under the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq.

title: Miami Beach PD Accurint/PSDEX Data Sharing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) What Miami Beach PD systems are shared with LexisNexis Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC) or Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX)? Typically this includes CAD, RMS, Jail Booking systems, etc.
- Please Provide: A brief written description will suffice - however if that is not possible, please provide a document from the integration process that shows which system(s) contribute to AVCC/PSDEX
2) What data is contributed to AVCC/PSDEX - does it include 911 caller information or incident witness/victim information? 
- Please Provide: A brief written description will suffice. If that is not possible, please provide a specific API/XML doc or other technical specifications that show which data fields your agency contributes to AVCC/PSDEX. 
3) Are you able to audit how your agency's data is used by other agencies once it enters AVCC/PSDEX? 
- Please Provide: A short written description, however if that is not possible, please provide an audit log of other AVCC subscriber's searches of Miami Beach PD's data. Any 1-week period is sufficient. 

The following documents have already been reviewed and do not answer the above questions (they are being provided for context only):
A) City Council Agenda: September 21, 2020: https://d2kbkoa27fdvtw.cloudfront.net/cityftmyers/e5f6e0e05c1db33a49c44224412ded210.pdf
B) Information Sharing Agreement: https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691623/FDLE_Interagency_Agreement_-_LexisNexis_-_Accurint_Virtual_Crime_Center_-_LCA_and_CA__2019__-_FMPD.pdf
C) LexisNexis Terms & Conditions: https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691621/20190718014704_lnrsMasterTCsGovt_-_Fort_Myers.pdf
D) AVCC Subscription Schedule A (Fort Meyers): https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691622/AVCC_Schedule_A_-_Ft_Myers_PD_071719.pdf 
E) AVCC Addendum (Florida): https://legistarweb-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/attachment/pdf/691620/20190718014704_AVCC_FLORIDA_Addendum_Fort_Myers.pdf

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

Any and all records, logs, or sign-in sheets indicating the headcount, attendance, or list of attendees at the municipal building during the May 20, 2026 meeting.  
The official Maximum Occupancy Certification/Certificate of Occupancy for the Damascus Township meeting room located at 60 Conklin Hill Road, specifying the physical capacity limits.

Any and all written communications, including but not limited to emails, text messages, memos, and letters, dated from May 1, 2026, through the present, between or among Damascus Township Supervisors (Joseph Canfield, Scott Rutledge, and Steven Adams), Township Solicitor Jeffrey Treat, Zoning Officer Ed Lagarenne, and/or township staff regarding public attendance, space limitations, overcrowding, or the exclusion/denial of entry of the public at the May 20, 2026 meeting.

All exterior cameras/CCTV footage from 60 Conklin Hill Road for May 20, 2026, from 06:45PM to 08:00PM. Including the main entrance to the public meeting room.

Official reasoning as to why members of the public were denied entry into the May 20,2026, public meeting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

George Klein

title: Flock Safety FOIA

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Flock Safety FOIA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to the following records related to the Winston-Salem Police Department's Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) system:

1. Organization Audit in Flock. The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock. The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Data Sharing:
   a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the department shares ALPR data ("Flock networks shared with me")
   b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the department receives ALPR data
   c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the department shares hotlist information ("Hotlists that I'm sharing")
   d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the department receives hotlist information ("Hotlists shared with me")

4. All current contracts, MOUs, statements of work, purchase orders, and amendments between the City of Winston-Salem/WSPD and Flock Safety (Flock Group, Inc.) and/or Axon Enterprise, Inc. related to ALPR systems.

5. The written ALPR use policy adopted pursuant to N.C. Gen. Stat. § 20-183.32, and any related SOPs or general orders governing ALPR data access, retention, and sharing.

6. Semi-annual ALPR audit reports generated by the ALPR Program Manager, as referenced in the WSPD ALPR FAQ.

Please provide audit data in a structured, machine-readable format such as .csv or .xlsx. Per N.C. Gen. Stat. § 132-6.2(a), I elect to receive all records in their original electronic format. Do not produce physical copies.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Peircen Lorenz

title: Aggregate statistics on Business Physical Disaster Loans for residential rental properties, FY2019–FY2026

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Aggregate statistics on Business Physical Disaster Loans for residential rental properties, FY2019–FY2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

For each federal fiscal year from 2019 through 2025, plus FY2026 year-to-date through the most recent month for which complete data is available, aggregate statistics on Business Physical Disaster Loan (BPDL) applications where the damaged property was a residential rental:

-Number of applications received.
-Number approved, with total approved loan amount and total verified loss amount.
-Number declined or withdrawn.

Aggregate data only - no personally identifiable or loan-level records. If DCMS uses a different internal flag for rental-property loans, please use the closest equivalent and note which field was queried.

Format: Excel or CSV.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ryan Green

title: Public records request – Disposition of conditional offer of employment for Kelly E. Sargent

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Public records request – Disposition of conditional offer of employment for Kelly E. Sargent
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any records that reflect the final disposition of the conditional offer of employment made by the Albany Police Department to Kelly E. Sargent for the position of Police Officer, as set out in a conditional offer letter dated September 18, 2017 (for example, whether it resulted in a formal offer, was rescinded, lapsed with no final offer, or was declined or withdrawn by Mr. Sargent).

If that conditional offer was rescinded or a formal offer was not extended based on information discovered during pre‑employment screening (such as psychological, medical, drug screening, background investigation, or other adverse findings), I request any non‑exempt records that generally describe the reason or category of reason for that decision, while omitting or redacting detailed medical, psychological, or other confidential personal information as permitted or required by law.

I understand that some records may be exempt in whole or in part. To the extent you determine any requested records are exempt, please provide all reasonably segregable non‑exempt portions and cite the specific statutory basis for each withholding or redaction.

I am requesting these records as a member of the public to understand how this conditional offer was ultimately resolved. If fees will exceed $25, please let me know before processing. Please provide responsive records electronically, via email or through the MuckRock portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Drew Bee

title: Lathrop PD Accurint/PSDEX Data Sharing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Based on the February 12, 2024 City Council meeting minutes, it appears that Lathrop PD was authorized to enter into a "Memorandum of Understanding with the San Joaquin
County Sheriff and local law enforcement agencies to create a public safety data exchange Consortium". - We are requesting a copy of that signed MOU.
2) We are requesting any MOUs, contracts, addendums, or other legal documents signed directly between Lathrop PD and LexisNexis (and subsidiaries, including LexisNexis Risk Solutions, Accurint, AVCC, PSDEX, etc.). This may include (but is not limited to) a LexisNexis Consortium Sub -Agency Addendum. 
3) Is your agency a Participating Agency (contributing data to AVCC) or an Associate Member (read only)?. If you are a Participating Agency, can you provide documentation on what systems and fields are integrated with AVCC/PSDEX? This may include RMS, CAD, Jail Booking etc. and fields may be listed in an API/XML file or technical specifications. We are specifically looking to understand if 911 caller, victim, and witness information is shared with AVCC. 
4) Does your agency restrict what other AVCC subscribers can view your agency's contributions, or is your agency's data "generally" available to all AVCC subscribers, per Section VIII of the MOU?
5) If you contribute Lathrop PD data to AVCC, please provide audit logs of how that data has been searched/downloaded by other agencies. Specifically, we are looking for any access to Lathrop PD data by non-California police departments or federal agencies. 
6) Beyond the data contained in the link below, we are requesting any additional files, communications, or presentation material (if any) made for the February 12, 2024 City Council Meeting related to the sharing of Lathrop PD data with LexisNexis and other local, state, and federal AVCC subscribers. 

Reference Document: https://www.ci.lathrop.ca.us/sites/default/files/fileattachments/city_council/meeting/26197/2024-02-12_item_4.6_authorize_city_to_enter_into_an_mou_with_sjc_sheriff_and_local_law_enforcement_agencies_to_create_a_psdex_consortium.pdf

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: U.S. Responses to Foreign State-Sponsored Health Disinformation

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: U.S. Responses to Foreign State-Sponsored Health Disinformation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All memoranda, assessments, and analytical reports addressing foreign state-sponsored health disinformation campaigns, including to disinformation related to COVID-19 vaccines, pandemic origins, or public health measures, attributed to or associated with the Russian Federation or Internet Research Agency (Агентство интернет-исследований) or the People's Republic of China, from January 1, 2020 through December 31, 2023.

All records reflecting U.S. diplomatic responses to identified foreign state-sponsored health disinformation, including any formal protests, demarches, or multilateral coordination efforts.

Policy frameworks, guidelines, or internal strategies governing the U.S. government's response to foreign state-sponsored health disinformation, including records that address the distinction between countering foreign disinformation and restrictions on domestic speech.

Records of interagency coordination, including with the NSC, DHS, HHS, and the intelligence community, regarding attribution of health disinformation to foreign state actors and the formulation of responsive measures.

Communications with NATO and the EU regarding coordinated responses to foreign health disinformation campaigns.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Elizabeth Clemons
